Title: A Familiar Ring to It: Biosynthesis of Plant Benzoic Acids

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for export of salicylic acid from plastids; loss causes SA accumulation in plastids and altered defense capacity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EDS5 exports SA from plastids after synthesis. 2. EDS5 expression is induced by stresses that trigger SA synthesis. 3. In eds5 mutants engineered to overproduce SA, SA accumulates in plastids, indicating transport is limiting.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; induced expression; subcellular metabolite accumulation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low under normal conditions; induced during pathogen attack or UV-C treatment
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to manipulate SA distribution and defense signaling, but may strongly affect stress responses
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transport step emphasizes subcellular compartmentation in BA metabolism.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Salicylic acid glucosides; p-aminobenzoic acid glucose conjugates
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Glucosylates SA on the hydroxyl group to produce SAG and also acts on pABA carboxyl group for storage forms
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT74F1 glucosylates SA to SA 2-O-β-glucoside. 2. It also conjugates glucose to the carboxyl group of pABA. 3. These reactions generate likely storage/inactivation forms in the vacuole.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Broad substrate specificity in BA conjugation.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: SA glucosides; MeSAG; salicyloyl glucose ester; pABA glucose conjugates
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Glucosylates SA on carboxyl group to form SGE and to a lesser extent on hydroxyl group; also acts on pABA
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT74F2 glucosylates SA to salicyloyl glucose ester and contributes weakly to SAG formation. 2. Overexpression of AtSGT1/UGT74F2 results in MeSA 2-O-β-glucoside formation. 3. It also glucosylates pABA on the carboxyl group.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also referred to as AtSGT1 in the review.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Salicyloyl glucose ester; pABA glucose conjugates
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Glucosylates carboxyl groups of SA and pABA for conjugated storage forms
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT75B1 glucosylates SA on its carboxyl group to produce SGE. 2. It also glucosylates pABA on its carboxyl group.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: SA amino acid conjugates; 4-substituted benzoic acid conjugates
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes amino acid conjugation of pABA-related or 4-substituted BA substrates; physiological significance remains unclear
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PBS3 is a GH3-like acyl adenylase/thioester-forming enzyme. 2. It conjugates pABA-related and 4-substituted benzoic acids with amino acids. 3. The physiological significance of some conjugates is unclear.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review distinguishes PBS3 from SA-conjugating GH3.5.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: methylated benzoic acid derivative; volatile benzenoid
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Methylates salicylic acid to methyl salicylate, increasing membrane permeability and volatility
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtBSMT1 is a BA/SA carboxyl methyltransferase from Arabidopsis. 2. It converts SA to MeSA, a more membrane-permeable and volatile derivative.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: methylated benzoic acid derivative; volatile benzenoid
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts salicylic acid to methyl salicylate in petunia
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Petunia BSMT1 is a BA/SA carboxyl methyltransferase. 2. It participates in MeSA formation from SA.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: methylated benzoic acid derivative; volatile benzenoid
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts salicylic acid to methyl salicylate in petunia
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Petunia BSMT2 is a BA/SA carboxyl methyltransferase contributing to MeSA biosynthesis.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Necessary for import of phenylpropanoid β-oxidative precursors into peroxisomes, thereby supporting BA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Reverse genetics shows PXA1 is required for import of β-oxidative phenylpropanoid precursors into peroxisomes. 2. PXA1 has intrinsic ATP-stimulated acyl-CoA thioesterase activity. 3. Transported substrate may be acyl-CoA on cytosolic side followed by cleavage and reactivation inside peroxisomes.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Substrate form during transport remains unresolved.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions in formation of SA-amino acid conjugates
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.5 is one of two Arabidopsis GH3-like proteins demonstrated to form amino acid conjugates with SA.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Conjugates DHBAs with xylose to generate xylosides involved in inactive storage or defense signaling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT89A2 produces DHBA xylosides such as 2,3-DHBX. 2. DHBA sugar conjugates may function as inactive storage forms or defense-related signaling compounds.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Various plants; bacterial feedback-insensitive DAHPS used experimentally
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Shikimate pathway; benzoic acid precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step of shikimate-derived and phenylalanine-derived benzoic acid network
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses phosphoenolpyruvate and D-erythrose 4-phosphate to form DAHP, initiating the shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flux into shikimate-derived metabolites and benzoic acid products; reduced DAHPS1 lowers floral benzenoid emission
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DAHPS catalyzes the shikimate-pathway entry reaction that partially controls flux toward plant benzoic acids. 2. Feedback-insensitive bacterial DAHPS overexpression in Arabidopsis increased shikimate and benzoic acid-derived products. 3. RNAi downregulation of petunia DAHPS1 reduced floral benzenoid emission.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developmentally regulated DAHPS1 is associated with petunia floral benzenoid emission; inducible under defense demand
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream flux-control target to elevate benzoic acid-derived metabolites, but broad shikimate-pathway perturbation may cause pleiotropic metabolic shifts
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad metabolic reprogramming due to increased aromatic amino acid and shikimate-derived metabolite flux
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review identifies DAHPS as a partially controlling entry point rather than a BA-specific enzyme.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Chorismate-derived benzoic acid pathway; salicylic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes chorismate to isochorismate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes salicylic acid biosynthesis; ICS1 mutation causes SA deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis sid2 SA deficiency results from mutation in ICS1. 2. ICS1 biochemically converts chorismate to isochorismate. 3. ICS1 is the major ICS contributing to SA production in Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling under biotic and abiotic stress
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review highlights ICS pathway as major route for SA in Arabidopsis.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Chorismate-derived benzoic acid pathway; salicylic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes chorismate to isochorismate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Minor contribution to downstream isochorismate-derived products relative to ICS1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis contains a second plastidial ICS encoded by ICS2. 2. Its contribution to downstream products is much less significant than ICS1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic and biochemical characterization cited in review
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as summarized core gene in review because it is specifically discussed as an isochorismate synthase paralog.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts isochorismate to ortho-succinylbenzoic acid in phylloquinone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for production of ortho-succinylbenzoic acid en route to phylloquinone
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In plants, isochorismate is converted to ortho-succinylbenzoic acid by PHYLLO. 2. This links chorismate-derived benzoic acid metabolism to phylloquinone synthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates salicylic acid to form 2,3-dihydroxybenzoic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes conversion of SA to 2,3-DHBA; loss of S3H abolishes detectable 2,3-DHBA and 2,3-DHBX
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. S3H was identified as the enzyme converting SA to 2,3-DHBA. 2. s3h knockout plants lack detectable 2,3-DHBA and 2,3-DHBA-xyloside.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Confirms 2,3-DHBA formation via SA rather than directly from isochorismate in Arabidopsis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transaminates prephenate to arogenate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies phenylalanine precursor via arogenate pathway for downstream benzoic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PPA-AT converts prephenate to arogenate in the plastidial arogenate route to phenylalanine. 2. This pathway feeds phenylalanine-derived benzoic acid biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Dehydrates and decarboxylates arogenate to phenylalanine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates phenylalanine precursor for benzoic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ADT catalyzes conversion of arogenate to phenylalanine in plastids. 2. Phenylalanine feeds the phenylpropanoid route to benzoic acids.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transaminates phenylpyruvate to phenylalanine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports cytosolic phenylalanine formation for benzoic acid precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identification of plant PPY-AT revealed that phenylalanine from the alternative phenylpyruvate pathway is formed in the cytosol. 2. This provides an additional precursor route into phenylalanine-derived benzoic acid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization; transient screens cited in review

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana tabacum; Oryza sativa; Solanum tuberosum; Silene latifolia
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step to phenylalanine-derived benzoic acids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates phenylalanine to cinnamic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Benzoic acid; salicylic acid; benzenoid compounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates phenylalanine-derived BA production; reduced PAL activity lowers SA accumulation in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL initiates the phenylpropanoid pathway by converting phenylalanine to cinnamic acid. 2. Tracer studies support SA synthesis from phenylalanine via cinnamic acid and BA. 3. Arabidopsis quadruple pal mutants with only 10% PAL activity contain 50% less SA than wild type.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; isotope labeling; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review highlights unresolved cross-talk between PAL and ICS routes to SA.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates cinnamic acid to cinnamoyl-CoA for entry into peroxisomal β-oxidative shortening
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates β-oxidative benzoic acid formation from cinnamic acid
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ph-CNL was identified as the enzyme that activates cinnamic acid to cinnamoyl-CoA in petunia. 2. It functions in the core β-oxidative route from CA to BA-CoA.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates cinnamic acid to cinnamoyl-CoA for β-oxidative BA formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes β-oxidative benzoic acid biosynthesis in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BZO1 is the Arabidopsis homolog of petunia Ph-CNL. 2. It participates in the core β-oxidative route from CA to BA-CoA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homolog identification; functional characterization summarized in review

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: 4-coumarate CoA ligase-like enzyme for p-coumarate β-oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates p-coumaric acid to p-coumaroyl-CoA to initiate β-oxidative shortening to 4-hydroxybenzoic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates β-oxidative side-chain shortening toward 4HBA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. At4g19010 catalyzes activation of p-coumaric acid in Arabidopsis. 2. This initiates β-oxidative shortening of p-CA to 4HBA.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrates cinnamoyl-CoA to 3-hydroxy-3-phenylpropanoyl-CoA and then oxidizes it to 3-oxo-3-phenylpropanoyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes core β-oxidative shortening steps toward benzoic acid; also accepts p-coumaroyl-CoA in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhCHD is a bifunctional hydratase/dehydrogenase in petunia β-oxidative BA biosynthesis. 2. It catalyzes hydration and oxidation steps from CA-CoA to the β-keto thioester intermediate. 3. It also uses p-coumaroyl-CoA in vitro.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Abnormal inflorescence meristem 1 / cinnamoyl-CoA hydratase-dehydrogenase homolog
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: β-keto thioester intermediate en route to BA-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes hydratase/dehydrogenase reactions in β-oxidative side-chain shortening
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Salicylic acid; hydroxybenzoylated choline esters; benzoyloxyglucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AIM1 knockout reduces SA, HBACs, and 3-/4-benzoyloxyglucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AIM1 is the Arabidopsis homolog of petunia PhCHD. 2. Loss of AIM1 decreases levels of SA, HBACs, and 3- and 4-BZOs, supporting its role in β-oxidative BA synthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves the β-keto thioester intermediate to produce benzoyl-CoA and acetyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completes β-oxidative shortening to benzoyl-CoA in petunia
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhKAT1 catalyzes the final cleavage step of the β-oxidative route to BA-CoA. 2. It releases acetyl-CoA while forming benzoyl-CoA.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes thiolytic cleavage in β-oxidative benzoic acid formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: KAT2 knockout reduces HBACs and 3-/4-BZOs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KAT2 is the Arabidopsis homolog of PhKAT1. 2. Knockout of KAT2 reduces BA-derived HBACs and benzoyloxyglucosinolates, supporting a role in β-oxidative BA biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Hydratase/lyase with similarity to cysteine proteinases
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Vanilla planifolia; Nicotiana tabacum; Hordeum vulgare
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: CoA-independent non-oxidative benzoic acid derivative pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Directly converts ferulic acid to vanillin with acetate release via hydratase/lyase activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes vanillin formation; expression in tobacco and barley leads to vanillyl alcohol glucoside accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The Vanilla planifolia 4HBD synthase was redefined as VpVAN. 2. VpVAN directly converts ferulic acid to vanillin. 3. Heterologous expression in tobacco and barley produced vanillyl alcohol glucoside.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purification; biochemical assay; heterologous expression; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Original p-coumarate to 4-hydroxybenzaldehyde activity could not be confirmed.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes benzaldehydes to the corresponding benzoic acids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AAO4 promotes BA formation from benzaldehyde; aao4 seeds show reduced BA and 3-/4-BZOs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis AAO4 is a cytoplasmic aldehyde oxidase with highest activity toward benzaldehyde. 2. aao4 knockout seeds contain reduced benzoic acid and benzoyloxyglucosinolates.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes benzaldehyde substrates to benzoic acids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports oxidation of benzaldehydes to benzoic acids in mitochondria
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RF2A from maize is an ALDH capable of oxidizing benzaldehyde substrates. 2. It exemplifies the mitochondrial ALDH route for BA formation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes benzaldehyde to benzoic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression elevates BA and BA-derived volatile emission in petunia flowers
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Snapdragon BALDH oxidizes benzaldehyde substrates and is localized to mitochondria. 2. Overexpression in petunia increases BA levels and BA-derived volatiles.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: 2-hydroxylates benzoic acid to form salicylic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts BA to SA in tobacco defense responses
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BA2H activity was partially purified from TMV-inoculated tobacco leaves. 2. It is a soluble P450 monooxygenase converting BA to SA. 3. BA-glucose may be the actual substrate, but the gene has not been isolated.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene identity remains unknown; enzyme included because the review treats it as a key missing molecular player.
